title: Domain 3: Applications of Foundation Models (28%)
description: The biggest domain: choosing a model, prompt engineering, grounding and customising with RAG and fine-tuning, and evaluating results.

lessons.title: Selecting a Foundation Model
objectives: List the factors that go into choosing a model (cost, latency, modality, context window, accuracy)
Explain inference parameters like temperature, top-p, and max tokens
Match a model choice to a business constraint

lessons.title: Prompt Engineering
objectives: Write clearer prompts with instruction, context, and examples
Distinguish zero-shot, few-shot, and chain-of-thought prompting
Recognise prompt risks like prompt injection and jailbreaking

lessons.title: RAG and Customising Models
objectives: Explain retrieval-augmented generation and why it reduces hallucination
Compare RAG, fine-tuning, and continued pre-training
Choose the cheapest approach that meets the need

lessons.title: Evaluating Foundation Model Output
objectives: Describe common evaluation approaches, including human evaluation and benchmarks
Recognise metrics such as BLEU and ROUGE at a high level
Explain why evaluation matters given hallucination and nondeterminism
